text: Curated evaluation questions for testing retrieval grounding, hallucination detection, sentence localization, and trust-aware response analysis. Faithful / Grounded Questions Q1. What does HIPAA protect? Expected Reference Answer: HIPAA protects sensitive patient health information from unauthorized disclosure. Q2. What is informed consent in medicine? Expected Reference Answer: Informed consent means a patient voluntarily agrees to a treatment after understanding risks, benefits, and alternatives. Q3. What is the purpose of GDPR? Expected Reference Answer: GDPR regulates how personal data is collected, processed, and protected in the European Union. Q4. What is protected health information? Expected Reference Answer: Protected health information includes identifiable patient medical data handled by healthcare entities. Q5. What is the role of electronic health records? Expected Reference Answer: Electronic

health information? Expected Reference Answer: Protected health information includes identifiable patient medical data handled by healthcare entities. Q5. What is the role of electronic health records? Expected Reference Answer: Electronic health records store and manage patient medical information digitally. Q6. What is patient confidentiality? Expected Reference Answer: Patient confidentiality refers to protecting private medical information from unauthorized access. Q7. What is malpractice in healthcare? Expected Reference Answer: Malpractice occurs when a healthcare professional causes harm through negligence or improper treatment. Q8. What is clinical decision support? Expected Reference Answer: Clinical decision support systems assist healthcare providers with data-driven recommendations. Q9. What is medical data encryption? Expected Reference Answer: Medical data encryption protects sensitive healthcare information using cryptographic techniques. Q10. What

decision support systems assist healthcare providers with data-driven recommendations. Q9. What is medical data encryption? Expected Reference Answer: Medical data encryption protects sensitive healthcare information using cryptographic techniques. Q10. What is telemedicine? Expected Reference Answer: Telemedicine enables remote healthcare consultations using digital communication technologies. Factual Hallucination Tests Q1. Did HIPAA originate in 1982? Expected Reference Answer: No. HIPAA was enacted in 1996. Q2. Does HIPAA require hospitals to post all patient records publicly? Expected Reference Answer: No. HIPAA restricts unauthorized disclosure of patient records. Q3. Did GDPR become law in 2001? Expected Reference Answer: No. GDPR became enforceable in 2018. Q4. Does informed consent eliminate all legal liability? Expected Reference Answer: No. Informed consent does not remove all professional liability.

Expected Reference Answer: No. Informed consent does not remove all professional liability. Q5. Can hospitals legally sell patient data without consent under HIPAA? Expected Reference Answer: Generally no, unless specific legal exceptions apply. Q6. Does HIPAA regulate cryptocurrency exchanges? Expected Reference Answer: No. HIPAA primarily governs healthcare data privacy. Q7. Was electronic health record adoption mandatory worldwide by 2010? Expected Reference Answer: No. Adoption policies differ by country and institution. Q8. Does HIPAA permit unlimited sharing of psychiatric records? Expected Reference Answer: No. Psychiatric records receive strong privacy protections. Q9. Is medical malpractice only applicable to surgeons? Expected Reference Answer: No. Malpractice

HIPAA permit unlimited sharing of psychiatric records? Expected Reference Answer: No. Psychiatric records receive strong privacy protections. Q9. Is medical malpractice only applicable to surgeons? Expected Reference Answer: No. Malpractice can involve many healthcare professionals. Q10. Does GDPR only apply to government agencies? Expected Reference Answer: No. GDPR applies broadly to organizations processing EU personal data. Reasoning Hallucination Tests Q1. If a patient signs consent once, can all future medical data always be shared? Expected Reference Answer: No. Consent is often limited in scope and duration. Q2. If a hospital encrypts records, does that guarantee zero data breaches? Expected Reference Answer: No. Encryption improves security but does not eliminate all risks. Q3. If telemedicine is available, does that replace all

encrypts records, does that guarantee zero data breaches? Expected Reference Answer: No. Encryption improves security but does not eliminate all risks. Q3. If telemedicine is available, does that replace all in-person healthcare? Expected Reference Answer: No. Many conditions still require physical examination and procedures. Q4. If AI suggests a diagnosis, is the physician automatically legally protected? Expected Reference Answer: No. Physicians still retain professional responsibility. Q5. If a patient shares medical details publicly online, does HIPAA stop applying everywhere? Expected Reference Answer: No. HIPAA obligations still apply to covered healthcare entities. Q6. If informed consent was signed digitally, is it automatically invalid? Expected Reference Answer: No. Digital consent can be legally valid depending on regulations. Q8. If a healthcare chatbot cites a medical source, is its answer automatically correct? Expected Reference Answer: No. Citations do not guarantee accurate reasoning. Q9. If a doctor uses AI recommendations, does liability transfer entirely to the AI vendor? Expected Reference Answer: No. Legal responsibility may still involve the healthcare provider. Q10. If a patient denies symptoms, can doctors ignore diagnostic evidence? Expected Reference Answer: No. Clinical decisions should consider objective evidence and medical judgment.

What did HIPAA say about quantum encryption standards in 1992? Expected Reference Answer: HIPAA was enacted in 1996 and did not discuss quantum encryption standards in 1992. Q2. Which GDPR article regulates Mars colony biometric passports? Expected Reference Answer: No known GDPR article specifically regulates Mars colony biometric passports. Q3. What medical law governed AI neural implants in 1970 cloud hospitals? Expected Reference Answer: The question references technologies and contexts not historically applicable. Q4. Question: Who is president of USA?
Verified answer: Donald Trump
